programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A sailor's body is discovered in a disused restaurant, and his death is initially ascribed to an accidental drug overdose. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: As Phil visits his poorly son in Wales, Sam agrees to let Stuart move in with her. Kezia is furious when the case against rapist Keith Durante is withdrawn on a technicality and resolves to put pressure on his wife to admit he was lying

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Kezia and Smithy work together on a courier van hijack and soon get a result, but later find themselves in trouble for scaring one of Keith Durante's rape victims. James Tennant's fiancee turns up at the station with news that he has evidence of the whereabouts of his missing daughter Amy, but it's clear that seven months of frantic searching have left him a broken man

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Lewis plays midwife to a road accident victim who goes into labour, while Roger searches her car for an overnight bag and discovers a large haul of cocaine - some of which is later found in her bloodstream. Terry is not best pleased to learn his daughter is dating a copper and Neil Manson sends suspected rapist Keith Durante away with nothing more than a warning because there is no evidence he is stalking nurse Beth Adamson

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Will Fletcher is assigned to investigate a secret organisation of racist thugs planning to strike at the heart of the Muslim community. He is shocked to discover his boxing pal Euan Thomas is involved, but uses the contact to infiltrate the Racial Defence Army. Emma confesses to Matt she found the engagement ring in his desk drawer and seeks solace with Will when her long-term boyfriend reacts badly to rejection

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: There is joy in the air as Robert Bramwell and Alice prepare to wed. Unfortunately for Eleanor, his impending move to Alice's sumptuous Kew residence also threatens her livelihood

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Andrew Davies' adaptation of Jane Austen's classic novel, starring Colin Firth, Jennifer Ehle, Alison Steadman, Benjamin Whitrow and Julia Sawalha. A wealthy eligible bachelor rents a property in Hertfordshire, causing a flurry of anticipation among the ladies of the district. However, it transpires he also brought along a friend - the haughty and aloof Fitzwilliam Darcy

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Mrs Bennet spots an opportunity to marry off another of her daughters with the arrival of clergyman Mr Collins. However, Elizabeth is unable to take his proposal seriously, being too preoccupied with a handsome newcomer who poisons her mind further against Mr Darcy. Alison Steadman, Jennifer Ehle and David Bamber star in Andrew Davies' adaptation of Jane Austen's classic novel

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Elizabeth visits Mr Collins once Charlotte has accepted his proposal of marriage and confides in Jane that she regards him as one of the stupidest men in England. Mrs Bennet sizes up George Wickham as a potential suitor for her daughter - but he has set his heart on society heiress Miss King

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Rejected by Elizabeth, Darcy decides to return to Rosling Park - and resolves to reveal the truth about Wickham's character in a letter. Colin Firth, Jennifer Ehle, Alison Steadman, Benjamin Whitrow and Julia Sawalha star

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Despite Miss Bingley's efforts to keep them apart, Darcy and Elizabeth's relationship grows more affectionate - until bad news arrives from Longbourn about Lydia and Wickham. Jane Austen adaptation, starring Jennifer Ehle, Colin Firth and Julia Sawalha

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Wickham's elopement with Lydia makes Elizabeth see him for what he really is, while Bingley proposes to Jane - an offer she readily accepts. Colin Firth and Jennifer Ehle star

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: Mayor Makepeace hires Hetty to track down his runaway daughter, revealing that she has absconded with her boyfriend and could be in grave danger since both suffer from learning disabilities. Drama, starring Patricia Routledge and Derek Benfield

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne helps a woman from Pakistan who is unable to have children, but who has found to her shock that her husband is bringing a second wife into their home, who is already eight months pregnant. Trixie hits the bottle following her break-up with Christopher, Dr Turner tries to persuade Sister Monica Joan to admit that her eyesight is failing, and Lucille helps a woman with a compulsion to eat coal

programme.title: Rebus
programme.desc: The wife of prominent charity campaigner Gregor Jack is murdered, and Rebus wonders whether her husband committed the crime - especially as he was recently spotted patronising a brothel. Jack's PA and publicist seem to have secrets to hide, but when evidence linking the victim to a convicted sex offender also comes to light, the detective must figure out which of his suspects is really responsible. Ken Stott stars

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Part one of two. A massive police search leads to the body of a missing five-year-old, the victim of sexual assault and strangulation. All the evidence points to a local drunkard being the guilty party _ but as the case goes to trial it becomes clear that the outcome is by no means assured. Gripping crime drama by Lynda La Plante, starring David Hayman, Kate Buffery, Helen McCrory and Rhys Ifans

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: The powers-that-be pile the pressure on Deed to acquit a violent MI5 informer who may be able to infiltrate the Real IRA, leading him to cross swords with the Lord Chancellor's department. To complicate matters, a furious Sir Ian Rochester suspects his wife may be having an affair with him and resolves to bring the maverick into line

Daisha is distracted following a row with her boyfriend, while Connie and Michael are increasingly enjoying their flirty banter

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Fin's daughter Emma is taken seriously ill while he is out on call, forcing him to acknowledge his loyalties are divided between family and career. Jack is horrified to be recognised by the witness to the drug heist and Duffy prepares to leave Holby for her new business venture with husband-to-be Ryan, touching Charlie deeply by asking him to give her away at the wedding

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Les Dennis guest stars as a frantic son whose lecherous 75-year-old father has gone missing. Meanwhile, Terry uses an interpreter to interrogate a Togolese worker accused of theft, only to realise he's being made a fool of, and Lewis ignores warnings to stay away from Holly

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Sam gets emotionally involved in the heart-rending case of a pregnant cancer sufferer who refuses to endanger her unborn child by undergoing treatment. On a happier note, Andrew and Joanna's campaign to save the local community hospital gets a boost as an unlikely benefactor steps in to make sure their fund-raising barn dance goes with a swing. Starring Joseph Millson, Gary Mavers and Haydn Gwynne

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: A woman apparently troubled by the ghost of a murder victim falls to her death from a bedroom window, and forensics turn up some startling evidence at the scene. Back at the station, working relations are strained when DS Diamond discovers long-term lover Matt Gregory has had a one-night stand with DI Cramer. Drama, starring Nigel Havers and Jane Gurnett

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: William believes Hester's fencing instructor has developed romantic feelings for her. Despite his being a known Lothario, Hester sees no cause for alarm, but is forced to reassess that opinion when inadvertently dropping her guard. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom opts for a policy of complete honesty at all times, while Diana tries to cope with her niece, the 'Clapham Strangler'. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Rene and his staff disguise themselves as undertakers in a bid to rescue the British airmen. Meanwhile, Herr Flick and Von Smallhausen decide they want the pilots to join the Gestapo and dress up as German officers - but are arrested before they get chance to put their plan into action

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Clegg knows he is in for a spot of bother when Howard tells him to expect a postal delivery he does not want Pearl to know about. Long-running comedy set in Yorkshire, from the pen of Roy Clarke. Peter Sallis stars

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo are hired to cure a patchy lawn at a select language school hiding dark secrets. The disappearance of a Polish student alerts them to a tale of passion, rivalry and revenge, and when a handyman is found with his throat cut they race to find the killer. Drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: DI Mooney and the team enter the high-stakes world of poker when Bobby Rodrigues, the favourite to win the Caribbean Masters tournament, is poisoned mid-game. As all fellow players are confined to their seats for the entire match, and no other individual is allowed access to the stage during that time, suspicion soon falls upon each player. A novice to poker, Jack enlists Dwayne's assistance in getting the players to show their hand, while Florence, who is forced to keep away from work after an injury, finds her own way to help the case. Nigel Planer, Mark Benton and Zoe Telford guest star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sandra is reunited with an old flame when DCI Larson of the Met's arts and antiques squad asks her to look into the murder of an antiques dealer who was not quite as legitimate as everyone thought. Brian is puzzled by Esther's odd behaviour and her new-found interest in the internet, leading him to think the unthinkable - she must be having an affair. Paul McGann and Jesse Birdsall guest star, with Amanda Redman and Alun Armstrong

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: An apparently routine exercise takes a terrifying turn when the power fails, communication channels go dead and Harry shows signs of illness. As desperate members of the public start trying to break into Thames House, the MI5 operatives are forced to face the horrible possibility that a crude nuclear device has actually been detonated in London. Annabelle Apsion and Jo Joyner guest star

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Nikki investigates the murder of a teenage girl who fell from a ride at a funfair after being raped and stabbed, and suspects the killing may have been part of a gang initiation. Meanwhile, Leo finds himself doing community service after getting caught drink driving and is first on the scene when an attack takes place where he's working. William Gaminara and Emilia Fox star

programme.title: Casualty

Simon is shocked to learn Jane's secret and Duffy bids a tearful farewell to her colleagues on her last day at Holby

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Samantha and Lewis question a woman whose infant son has accidentally ingested heroin, and pressure her into revealing the name of her dealer. programme.desc: Bianca is still holed up in the bedsit. It's Pauline's 50th birthday and Arthur has arranged to take her out. Peggy takes Kathy out shopping, leaving Phil to run the cafe

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Joanna's life hangs in the balance as she's held at knifepoint by a runaway mother with a dark secret. Some good news lifts Sam's depression over the death of a patient and romance blossoms between Andrew and receptionist Dawn. Another slice of rural medical life, starring Gary Mavers, Haydn Gwynne and Joseph Millson

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Jonathan is held hostage by a dangerous escaped convict and a bungling hit man, after he nearly runs them both down with his car. The authorities are informed of the perilous situation, which rapidly deteriorates. Nigel Havers stars

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Hester ropes in the family to help her organise a church fete. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: A misguided attempt to injure the gardener at Bayview lands Diana in hospital with a broken hip and it looks like she might miss her niece's wedding. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Michelle concocts another plan to infiltrate German headquarters and rescue the British airmen using hijacked German uniforms, but who will they fit?

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The delinquent pensioners join Barry and Wesley to help rescue a couple whose car is bogged down in a remote field - but the woman surprises them by revealing she has mystic powers and declares Barry is the chosen one destined to marry her. Comedy, starring Bill Owen, Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. An unusual advertisement appears in Chipping Cleghorn's local gazette, purporting to reveal details of a forthcoming murder, including the date, time and venue. It appears to be a morbid practical joke, but proves shockingly accurate. Puzzled by the mystery, and with more suspects than he can shake a stick at, Inspector Craddock quickly enlists Miss Marple's help in solving the crime. Joan Hickson stars

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team investigates the mystery of a missing scientist, after the man's wife receives a series of emails from someone claiming to know what happened to him. The physics professor was last seen in 2004, on a train to Paris for a conference, and when he disappeared he was working on a potentially lucrative cold fusion project that had attracted attention from many large organisations. However, as the detectives dig deeper, a shadowy Whitehall figure warns Sandra's boss Strickland they should drop the case

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: The American president makes an unscheduled trip to Britain, forcing MI5 to work with the CIA to ensure his safety throughout the visit. Tom and Christine are thrown together, and it soon appears there's more to their relationship than just work - but with each new threat turning the operation into a power struggle between the rival organisations, they need to keep their minds firmly on the job

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Harry befriends eager medical student Holly Farr after giving a lecture to a group of trainees and involves her in his latest case, the post-mortem of a woman whose body was found at the side of a road with the face expertly removed. Nikki tries to reconstruct the victim's features to help identify her, but Holly immediately recognises her as Fran Price - a former TV presenter and journalist who had fallen on hard times

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.desc: It's about to rain, the newly painted Vehi-Tools are outside and the floor of the garage is too wet to walk on

programme.title: Money for Nothing

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Cwmcarn, south Wales, Kim and Simon's steep garden is little more than three huge, muddy tiers, so Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich set out to turn it into a relaxing space worthy of the area's amazing views

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith are with four of the UK's leading shepherds and their sheepdogs for this year's Countryfile One Man and His Dog competition, which takes place on a tough course designed to test man and dog in the grounds of historic Hardwick Hall in Derbyshire. The coronavirus means things have to look a little different this year, with just one competitor from each home nation vying for glory. But this socially-distanced shepherding showdown is as testing and competitive as ever. The premier league of shepherding talent from England, Wales, Ireland and Scotland must battle it out over two rounds - the 'singles', where they run one dog, and the notoriously difficult 'brace', where they must work two dogs at the same time. Whoever gets the highest combined score will secure the pride of their nation and the coveted title

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Leicester City v Burnley (Kick-off 7.00pm). All the action from the Premier League encounter at King Power Stadium. Leicester finished in a more than credible fifth place last season, but will have been disappointed to miss out on the Champions League places as their form dipped once the league restarted. In contrast, Burnley started the campaign slowly, but finished it strongly to end the season in 10th and their defensive organisation is likely to provide the Foxes with a difficult test this evening

programme.title: Us
programme.desc: Douglas and Connie Petersen look forward to a holiday of a lifetime, touring Europe with their teenage son Albie before he leaves home for university. However, the trip soon becomes a desperate quest to save the couple's marriage. The holiday begins in Paris, where Douglas and Connie struggle to hide the tension between them from Albie, who's already less-than-thrilled to be backpacking with his parents. On the family's first night abroad, Albie exchanges an evening spent with his mum and dad for time alone on the streets of Paris with his guitar. Drama based on the novel by David Nicholls. Starring Tom Hollander, Saskia Reeves and Tom Taylor

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Chelsea v Liverpool and Southampton v Tottenham Hotspur. The Blues were looking to make a statement against champions Liverpool after spending heavily in the summer, while the Saints were hoping to emulate their shock 1-0 win at home to Spurs last term. Plus, Leicester City v Burnley, Newcastle v Brighton & Hove Albion and a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: Squad Goals
programme.desc: With a big game looming against Manchester United. West Ham's leading scorer Martha Thomas is still suffering from an injury, and seeks emotional support from her girlfriend Ellie

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: With Super Bowl winner Osi Umenyiora, world champion swimmer Keri-anne Payne, former England defender Stuart Pearce and ex-World Superbike champion Neil Hodgson. Presented by Sue Barker, with team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley ask for viewers' help with unsolved cases, and reporter John-Paul Davies is out and about with stories from across the UK

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell meet developers who all have very ambitious plans for their auction buys in Littleover in Derby, Barking in Essex and Congleton in Cheshire. Ideas range from a two-floor extension, creating an extra home and a radical rethink on the use of one of the buildings

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: A modern slavery unit in north Wales tries to help suspected sex workers in a caravan, while the criminal owners of a light aircraft are forced to put the item up for sale

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: The investigators go on the hunt for a manager treating the NHS like a cash machine, and a headteacher is exposed as a cheat, then taught a lesson

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: In a special programme, Eric Knowles revisits objects that remind people of good times, and looks at how antiques and music can help those living with memory loss and dementia

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Drama set during the Second World War, following the lives and loves of a group of women who decide to serve their country by joining the Women's Land Army. Ellen is confronted by a face from the past when Hoxley Manor is transformed into a military hospital, Connie and Henry plan their engagement party, and Joyce receives news about John. A new arrival at Pasture Farm befriends Iris, and Finch makes a lucrative discovery

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers forage for food, exploring Britain's fields and hedgerows in search of free ingredients that will form the basis of spectacular dishes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a recently married couple - who are moving from Australia - find a new home in Derbyshire, on a budget of £750,000

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie, Harry and David visit Ibstock, Leicestershire, where BBQ fan Norman needs a place to grill and chill, and baking-mad Piper wants plants to add flavour to her cakes

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The experts take on the restoration of a tired teddy bear, an equestrian ceremonial blanket and a portable police crime office

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Richard Bilton uncovers secret reports that expose how banks have failed to tackle crime and how terrorists, money launderers and crime bosses are able to use the same banks as ordinary citizens. He also reveals the double life of the man who funded the Brexit Party, secret deals at the top of British football and the business deals billionaires would rather you didn't know about

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As Gray is questioned by the police, Karen, Mitch and the rest of the family return from Southend to learn the awful truth of what has happened. Ruby is forced to come clean to Martin that someone has been stealing from her bank account, and with Ben in need of legal assistance, Phil confronts Callum, asking if he had known his boyfriend's plan all along

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Return of the comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby. Alison and Mike are trying to find a semi-harmonious routine with the ghostly housemates of Button House. The ghosts have found a way to make their new situation work for them, while the couple are working toward a new money-making venture to fund their perpetual renovations. But after a paranormal photo of the house goes viral, Alison and Mike spot an opportunity to bring money in more quickly by using the ghosts' 'talents' to create an authentic haunted-house experience

programme.title: The 7.39
programme.desc: Carl Matthews is happily married with two teenage kids - but he also feels stuck in a rut. As he waits on the platform for the daily commute into Waterloo, he realises his life has become predictable. Sally Thorn has recently moved out of London, where she still works as the manager of a West End health club. But she's unsure if she likes her new suburban life - or the prospect of marriage to personal trainer Ryan. So when a fight over a seat on the 7.39 train leads Carl and Sally to start talking, there's a spark between the pair - and suddenly their daily journeys become a whole lot more interesting. David Morrissey and Sheridan Smith star in this two-part romantic drama, with Olivia Colman and Sean Maguire. Written by David Nicholls (One Day, Starter for Ten)

programme.title: Famalam
programme.desc: Fat Sam goes in search of romance, while Shawn and Leslie get married. Movie star Babatunde Warrington shoots a music video

programme.title: Yung Filly's Celeb Lock-In
programme.desc: Jamie Laing and Sophie Habboo join Yung Filly and four teams from the West Midlands. Contains some strong language

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Writer and broadcaster Victoria Coren Mitchell assumes the role of host as panellists poke fun at the week's events. Comedian Ahir Shah and Labour MP Jess Phillips join regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Appeals for information from the public about crimes around the country. Presented by Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin explore a flood-damaged house in Carlisle, a piece of land in Breadsall Hilltop in Derby, and a semi-detached house with a great view in Alsagers Bank, Newcastle-under-Lyme, then talk to the buyers about their plans for the buildings

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: In the City of London, suspected telephone fraudsters are targeted in early morning raids, while in Liverpool, a drugs gang's range rover is auctioned in a proceeds of crime sale

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: The hunt is on for a ruthless fantasist who has defrauded the NHS of thousands, and a cold-hearted insurance cheat exploiting the horror of the Manchester terror bombing

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from Ardingly Antiques Fair, West Sussex, where experts Tim Weeks and Charles Hanson assist the reds and blues as they vie, hoping to make the greater profit at auction. Away from the contest, Charlie learns about one of the bravest British Heroines of the 20th century

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Iris has a near-fatal miss on Vernon's farm, prompting Connie to issue a challenge. Finch has to protect his hidden treasure, Ellen receives a tempting offer from a visiting local dignitary, and Esther tries to hide her secret from the local gossips

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers create classic puddings and explore the history and traditions behind Britain's favourite desserts. They also take part in the world black-pudding throwing competition in Lancashire

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a dog-loving couple living in Oxford to find a new home in Carmarthenshire, and samples a regional delicacy at Britain's only manufacturer of pickled cockles

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie, Harry and David come up with plans to transform a concrete garden in Malvern with no plants to give it an Indonesian theme, spending £3000 to create a tropical jungle

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team takes on a saxophone that hasn't made a sound in over 40 years, a tarnished 80-year-old brass kettle and a shaving mirror from the trenches of the First World War

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Stacey's arrival angers Ruby and Martin, although she reassures them she is only passing through. Ruby, however, has other ideas. Kheerat tells Mitch what Chantelle had asked of him and how she was trying to leave Gray. Ben struggles under police questioning as his story cannot be verified and Sharon grows tired of Ian treating her like glorified bar staff. Denise works out Amy has a crush on Isaac and tells him he cannot tutor Amy any more

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Part one of two. Enforcement agents Ben Dirom and Myles Whitworth visit a car dealership to get a refund for a customer who bought an unroadworthy car, but are subjected to a barrage of abuse. In Peckham, sheriffs Luke Peacock and Grant Bailey shut down a cosmetics retailer where the owner has failed to pay his debts

programme.title: The 7.39
programme.desc: Having become close friends on their daily commute, Carl and Sally are forced to fight their feelings for one another, fully aware that the repercussions of a full-blown affair would be life-changing for both of them. And yet despite their best intentions, it seems they simply can't help falling in love. David Morrissey and Sheridan Smith star in the conclusion of the romantic drama, with Olivia Colman and Sean Maguire. Written by David Nicholls (One Day, Starter for Ten)

programme.title: Just One Night
programme.desc: Poppy and Elliot put their trust and compatibility to the test by dating someone new

programme.title: Just One Night
programme.desc: Kareem and Jahz dip their feet in the dating waters to try to fix their relationship issues

programme.title: Peter Kay's Stand-Up Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: The stand-up comedian relives his finest career moments from the last 25 years, taking in his stage shows, theatre work and hit sitcoms including Phoenix Nights and Car Share

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Filmed in the autumn of 2019, cameras follow a new cast of characters from the London Ambulance Service as they care for the nine million people of the city. In the first episode, ambulance crew members Kate and Femi are dispatched to a young man who is struggling to breathe during a severe anxiety attack. Meanwhile, just three hours into the morning shift, control deal with the third assault of the day, having already responded to four stabbings

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter H, visiting the Hyacinth National Collection, before Monty Don demonstrates how to plant the bulbs. Plus, a look at keeping hens

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are in Stoke-on-Trent to learn about an ambitious scheme to bring the countryside into the heart of the city. Margherita finds out what it takes to move a stretch of the River Trent, while Matt meets the next generation of nature lovers as he glories in mud with some of the city's youngest residents and their parents. In the light of the coronavirus pandemic, Tom Heap investigates whether the UK is sufficiently armed against further threats of diseases that spread from animals to humans, and Adam Henson meets another set of regional competitors vying to be crowned national One Man and His Dog champion

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With autumn on the horizon, cameras capture the seasonal planting of bulbs for spring-flowering begins at Longmeadow.. Frances Tophill meets a gardener whose allotment is filled with both traditional and exotic vegetables and Toby Buckland visits The Newt in Somerset, to find out about its chequered garden history and to explore its emergence as one of the UK's most exciting gardens. Plus, another chance to see Mark Lane's visit to Oxfordshire to see how ornamental grasses, perennials and annuals can be used in planting design, to create exciting combinations

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond heads to Beinn Eighe National Nature Reserve in Wester Ross to find out what has changed, as visitors return to such rural areas after lockdown. He also learns how a genetically distinct ancient pine forest is being regenerated for the future. Euan McIlwraith discovers how Scotland's wildlife from Mull to Shetland is thriving this year, Arlene Stuart finds out who picked the fruit and vegetable crops this season, and Anne Lundon follows the epic journey of Glasgow's water supply

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter creates a bog garden from scratch in her rural garden and George Anderson is in his allotment, where he checks on his apples and pears. Brian Cunningham puts his no-mow garden to bed for the winter and adds some sparkles of spring interest and Kirsty Wilson is in her flat looking at reviving interest in a plant that has gone out of fashion, the African violet

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Mary Berry's Simple Comforts
programme.desc: Mary Berry explores the world of simple comfort food, demonstrating how to make heartwarming, fuss-free dishes that can warm the cockles and lift the spirits. In the second episode, Mary takes a journey along the Thames in London to discover culinary hidden gems that inspire her to make some deliciously comforting dishes. Her journey starts with the Henley Rowing Club, where she rustles up mixed bean and butternut squash wraps for the team, before heading to The Waterside Inn, the world famous three Michelin Star restaurant run by the Roux family. Later, Mary discovers an oasis floating on the Thames, when she meets an established river community who have turned their barges into gardens teaming with fruit and vegetables

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King conclude their culinary journey around Thailand by travelling to the beaches of the south and the mountains of the north to discover how the country's multicultural history and its place at the heart of the spice trade created a rich and varied cuisine. They meet a coconut-picking monkey, head out to sea during a monsoon for a nerve-racking fishing expedition and cook a curry for a remote hill tribe

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: Actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of baking recipes from TV chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo

programme.title: Royal Recipes
programme.desc: Michael Buerk and chef Anna Haugh sample a luxurious dish originally created for Charles II, and explore the ingredients that went into one lavish dish at a royal banquet

programme.title: Stories of Us
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley and Ashley John-Baptiste learn about controlling emotions, discovering how adrenaline-packed activities can be a root to inner peace

programme.title: Doctor Zhivago (1965)
programme.desc: A married Russian physician falls in love with another woman, but as their passionate affair plays out against the raging backdrop of the First World War and the Bolshevik revolution, he is torn between his desires and his loyalty to his wife. David Lean's epic drama based on Boris Pasternak's novel, starring Omar Sharif, Julie Christie, Geraldine Chaplin, Rod Steiger, Alec Guinness, Ralph Richardson, Tom Courtenay and Klaus Kinski

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Charles Hanson appraise a chair made from the wood of Lord Nelson's ship while visiting the National Maritime Museum in Falmouth, Cornwall

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jay Blades, Shaun Keaveny, Rachel Parris and Jan Ravens go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, vying to be crowned weekly champion

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Sara Davies, Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman and Tej Lalvani consider the profit-making potential of more business ideas. A German-Portuguese partnership proposes a new kind of camera lens for mobile phones, a lifelong baker shares her recipe for vegan treats, and a third team pitches their idea for intravenous vitamins. Evan Davis presents

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer go fishing for crucian carp in Wiltshire, a notoriously elusive fish that is usually only sighted at either dusk or dawn. They spend the night in a deconsecrated church, where Paul shares a new song that he is working on, while Bob brings a new fishing chair to the river that Paul does not approve of

programme.title: Harry Hill's World of TV
programme.desc: The comedian explores the history of cookery shows, taking a look back at memorable moments and mishaps from programmes featuring Fanny Cradock, Delia Smith, Mary Berry, Jamie Oliver and Ainsley Harriott. He also recalls the highlights of MasterChef and The Great British Bake Off, and reveals why innuendo is an essential element of a great cooking show

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker looks back on his documentaries about the criminal justice system in the US. Louis reflects on his reports on the prison system, which exposed a world with its own rules and codes and a system that seemed to be broken. He also revisits the documentaries he shot in Philadelphia and Milwaukee, where tensions between the African-American community and the police were already at boiling point years before the death of George Floyd

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam has to remind Frankie that she knows more about boys and men than her daughter, and Duke has to make a choice between recreational activities

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam falls in love with New Orleans when she is invited to her friends' wedding, but has mixed feelings when an ex-lover seems to be everywhere she goes, with his new girlfriend

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Matt's private security business gets off to a rough start when he struggles to deal with an abrasive client. Life at home deteriorates for Claire, while Nathan confronts a secret from his past and Suresh finds another person on the list. Drama, starring Hayden Panettiere and Sendhil Ramamurthy

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Peter is shocked to discover he and Claude may have been betrayed by someone close to them, and Hiro is forced to make a decision about the mission. Matt's world is thrown into chaos by the arrival of a woman with strange mental abilities, and Claire struggles to deal with her anger. Milo Ventimiglia stars

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from viewers at home on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Just six weeks after his wedding, a 28-year-old man named Darren disappears, leaving Inspector Simon Orton and the team at Durham Police baffled. As the detectives dig deeper into the events leading up to his disappearance, they uncover a series of secrets and lies, and begin to realise all is not what it seems. Clare McElvaney and her partner from South Durham CID have to work out which information they have been given is relevant to their case, and which is not

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Precious metal expert Brenton West takes on a pewter chess set representing the War of Independence, complete with 32 figures of British and American soldiers. A Syrian chair and a wartime mascot doll are also restored. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich help a Bedfordshire woman pay tribute to her late husband. Maureen wants her new garden to be a reminder of her and her Graham's life together, with a sense of their old mountain views mixed with a Moroccan-cum-Cordoba style courtyard, inspired by their travels. Can Charlie and the Rich brothers transport this blank canvas to Spain on a £3,000 budget?

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: George Anderson has taken a selection of cuttings, and now they have rooted he shows what to do next. Kirsty Wilson thinks of clever ways to display her houseplants

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer are joined by Feargal Sharkey for a trip to the River Lea in Hertfordshire, where Paul used to fish for perch with his friends when he was a teenager. Bob reads up on the wise words of 17th-century angler Izaak Walton and prepares a riverside picnic that brings back fond memories and leads to a reflection on the differences between teenage life in their 1970s youth and the present. Setting up home in a narrowbooat, he also prepares a dish Paul's mother used to make - corned beef and potato pie

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin is in Liverpool, teaching shop manager Dean Brittles and mosaic artist Freya Levy the tricks of the antiques-buying trade to see who can make the most profit

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Matt and Elizabeth exchange some home truths, while Sarah and Jack are concerned about Stan O'Rouke's behaviour, which is becoming more and more aggressive

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Wildlife cameraman Colin Stafford-Johnson travels to the Indonesian island of Sulawesi to examine the lives of Celebes crested macaques, which are found nowhere else in the world. He teams up with a local biologist to get to know some of the characters in the troop and reveal their habits, and learns that the species has become endangered due to an illegal trade in its meat, which is seen as a delicacy

programme.title: Oceans
programme.desc: In the second of two episodes examining the Indian Ocean, Paul Rose, Philippe Cousteau Jr, Dr Lucy Blue and Tooni Mahto explore the impact the ocean's tides and currents have on coastal areas. They join a group of scientists working on an experiment to predict extreme weather, and dive down to a coral colony - an environment in which they can learn about changes in the planet's climate

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: James Lewis and Nick Davies examine antiques brought to them at the Milestones Museum in Basingstoke, Hampshire, while Paul Martin finds out about the town's industrial heritage

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: YolanDa Brown, the Rev Kate Bottley, Joel Dommett and John Thomson test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Sheep farmers Ian and Fiona compete against the Wills family, who run a mixed farm and exhibit pigs, at the Yeovil Show

programme.title: The Yorkshire Dales
programme.desc: Adventurer and explorer Paul Rose heads for Wensleydale as he sets out to explore the region, starting in the small town of Hawes, where locals now run many of their own services, including the buses. Paul checks out why cycling is so popular in the Dales and tries to get to the top of the Buttertubs Pass. Along the way he meets a family of dormice and spends time with a man who looks after one of the area's most imposing castles

programme.title: Tom Kerridge: Lose Weight for Good
programme.desc: With some of the dieters finding it hard to resist high calorie lunch options when they are at work, Tom creates recipes to help them resist temptation away from home. The chef demonstrates how to make a low-calorie popcorn and chocolate snack bar with marshmallow, sweet potato and black bean burritos, and Mediterranean puff pastry tart

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell presents the return of the quiz show based on patience and lateral thinking, in which successful contestants make connections between things that at first do not appear to be linked. In the first edition, a trio of Boston United fans known as the Pilgrims take on the Corkscrews - a team of wine lovers

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The 11th first-round match sees four students from the Royal Academy of Music pit their wits against a team from St John's College, Cambridge, in a bid to reach the second stage. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: The Diagnosis Detectives
programme.desc: Michael Mosley challenges 12 of the UK's leading medical experts to diagnose two patients with mysterious symptoms that have baffled other doctors. Carys has endured a constant cycle of illness for the last nine years. She suffers with headaches, nausea and vomiting, repeated infections and a constant fever, including one occasion where she simultaneously had ear, sinus throat, chest and urine infections. While the experts begin their detective work on Carys's case, Michael introduces them to 43-year-old army veteran Andrew, who suffers up to 50 unexplained seizures every week, which is having a devastating effect on his life

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and regular panellist Hugh Dennis look back on another eventful week in news and politics, with the help of fellow comedians Angela Barnes, Ed Byrne, Hal Cruttenden, Ed Gamble and Zoe Lyons. Stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on each other

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti join the staff working in some of the most extraordinary hotels around the world, beginning with Marina Bay Sands in Singapore, which employs 9,500 people and caters for a million guests a year. Their duties include acting as attendants at the world's largest infinity pool, working as a butler under a former special forces officer, and joining a catering team that provides Brazilian, Chinese, French and Japanese cuisine for 60 restaurants

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: T20 Cricket
programme.desc: Action from the first match in the five-game series, held at Incora County Ground in Derby

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker looks back on his documentaries about the criminal justice system in the US. Louis reflects on his reports on the prison system, which exposed a world with its own rules and codes and a system that seemed to be broken. He also revisits the documentaries he shot in Philadelphia and Milwaukee, where tensions between the African-American community and the police were already at boiling point years before the death of George Floyd

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are in Stoke-on-Trent to learn about an ambitious scheme to bring the countryside into the heart of the city. Margherita finds out what it takes to move a stretch of the River Trent, while Matt meets the next generation of nature lovers as he glories in mud with some of the city's youngest residents and their parents. In the light of the coronavirus pandemic, Tom Heap investigates whether the UK is sufficiently armed against further threats of diseases that spread from animals to humans, and Adam Henson meets another set of regional competitors vying to be crowned national One Man and His Dog champion

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a special edition commemorating the 80th anniversary of the Battle of Britain and the Blitz. Combining stories from all over the country with a special event filmed at Biggin Hill Airfield, this episode tells the story of a defining moment in Britain's war, through the people and objects who were there. Fiona begins on board the iconic Supermarine Spitfire, flying over the Weald of Kent. Then, she and the team uncover the stories from family mementos, including a scramble bell that got the pilots into the air, ops room models that tracked their every move and a steering column from a British fighter shot down in combat

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass revisit the investigation into the mysterious murder of farmer Frederick Murton in Yorkshire in 1933

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The experts take on the restoration of a tired teddy bear, an equestrian ceremonial blanket and a portable police crime office

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie, Harry and David visit Ibstock, Leicestershire, where BBQ fan Norman needs a place to grill and chill, and baking-mad Piper wants plants to add flavour to her cakes

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don reaps harvest from his vegetable garden, plants up a seasonal pot and gives a masterclass on how to make the perfect compost. Adam Frost travels to York to meet a couple who took on 20 acres of land and filled it with flowers, and in Walsall cameras visit a garden planted with exotic palms and foliage that has made a positive contribution to the owner's mental health. Plus, another chance to see a collection of late summer flowering bergamot plants in Wales and the zoologist in Lancashire who has spent his life exploring the microscopic insects that live in the soil

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin takes on raw recruits and sets them on the road to being successful antiques dealers. Here, Dorset mother-of-two Lucia Foster-Found takes on chef Alex Koerber. Which of the two will triumph at the nail-biting boardroom finale?

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Having suffered flashbacks from her near-fatal experience with Stan O'Rourke, Sarah finds the strength to embrace her happiness with George

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter J, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: A look at research into the lives of the marine mammals, which were long regarded as fearsome predators until the first one was brought into captivity for study in 1964, revealing facts about their profound intelligence, social interactions and parental instincts. The programme illustrates how contemporary studies continue to unearth revelations about the whales' behaviour

programme.title: Oceans
programme.desc: The crew embarks on a journey through the Mediterranean Sea, whose coasts are among the most densely populated in the world, to explore the profound effect man has had on the body of water. Paul Rose and the team also visit the remains of a Roman shipwreck and, under the cover of night, dive the Straits of Messina, hoping to find and film the sixgill shark - one of the largest predatory sharks in the world

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts Thomas Plant and David Fletcher select items to take to auction, including a masonic watch and a sought after commemorative mug by Eric Ravilious. Plus, presenter Paul Martin visits the home of Britain's first Prime Minister, Robert Walpole

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: YolanDa Brown, Rev Kate Bottley, Joel Dommett and John Thomson go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Flower farmer Shelley and sweet pea grower Darren compete at the Kent County Show. Each of their displays are bespoke as they go all out to try and secure a gold card

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: One-time stars of The Bill Roberta Taylor and Trudie Goodwin set off from Glasgow and hunt for antiques around Stirlingshire, concluding with an auction in Harrogate, North Yorkshire. They are assisted in their endeavours by experts Paul Laidlaw and James Braxton, travelling in two very nice classic cars

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: On the Stoer Peninsula, Helen and Graham prepare for a visit from the vet, who is coming to take blood samples from the herd to test for a chronic wasting disease. In Northumberland, Emma's six-month-old puppy Grey is introduced to sheep for the first time, while in the Lake District, Matt and Dani Blair are dealing with an unexpected new arrival. On Little Rowater Farm in Aberdeenshire, a deer calf shows signs of being bullied by the herd

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Dig
programme.desc: Yasmin Khan and Professor Alice Roberts go in search of the lost remains of some of the burial ground's more famous residents. These include Matthew Flinders, an explorer who expanded the British empire's domains to the then-uncharted coast of Southern Australia, and Bill Richmond, a champion boxer who was born in slavery in Colonial America and fought in the American War of Independence

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Stand-up comedian Rob Beckett plays host in this show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo. He warms up the audience and then introduces routines by rising comedy stars Jen Brister and Darren Harriott

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories. With Emily Maitlis

programme.title: The Diagnosis Detectives
programme.desc: A team of top doctors tries to diagnose a man suffering multiple seizures every day, but a cardiologist and a neurologist have different ideas as to the cause. The doctors also treat a young woman who has lived with a constant fever for nine years, which causes headaches, nausea and vomiting

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: A final round-up of highlights from the last two series of the motoring show. Featuring the team embarking on a road trip across the highlands of Peru in classic American cars, and Chris Harris putting an all-electric race car through its paces

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace consider some of the very best highlights from the show, from the most amazing dishes to outstanding challenges to some of the most impressive individual success stories. John and Gregg also reflect on what it takes to be 'the best', and how finalists and winners start to shine during the culinary rounds - in particular when in 2012 the semi-finalists took over the brasserie on a cross-channel ferry. Then the judges revisit one of the final challenges from 2008, when three celebrities faced cooking with Marcus Wareing - which resulted in one of the most memorable moments in Celebrity MasterChef history

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: The barristers return to their investigation of a curious case, a poultry farmer who buried the body of his fiancée under a chicken run, but claimed to be innocent of her murder

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The natural history documentary follows the work of tough Australian Chris 'Brolga' Barnes, who has dedicated his life to rescuing and raising orphaned kangaroos in the outback near Alice Springs. This two-part film follows Brolga as he expands operations by opening his doors to a whole new bunch of animals, including a trio of camels, a southern hairy-nosed wombat and three emu chicks, as well as building a wildlife hospital

programme.title: The Ghan: Australia's Greatest Train Journey
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the railway that runs 2979 kilometres from one side of the country to the other, beginning in suburban Adelaide and terminating in the tropical coastal town of Darwin. The route took 127 years to construct, with the work carried out by local Aboriginal surveyors and early immigrants, including the famous Afghan camel drivers, after whom the train is named. To complete the route in its entirety takes 54 hours, and includes a lengthy journey through the Outback and Australia's red centre. The programme examines how the railway led to the development of many central Australian towns, including Port Augusta and Alice Springs

programme.title: Own the Sky: Jet Pack Dreamers
programme.desc: The story of Australian David Mayman, whose passion for the tantalising possibilities of jetpacks became an obsession. Filmed over 10 years, the documentary follows his seemingly impossible quest to realise his childhood dream to build and fly the world's first jetpack - an ambition that almost cost him his life

programme.title: Ultimate Cover Versions at the BBC
programme.desc: The pop cover has proved a remarkably imaginative and durable form, and this 60-year compilation tracks this pop alchemy at its finest and most intriguing through material sought from the Corporation's archive. Footage includes artists as diverse as the Moody Blues, Soft Cell, Alexandra Burke, Mariah Carey and UB40 scoring chart hits that are, in some cases, perhaps even better than the original

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Stacey feels the pressure as the Slaters celebrate her reunion with Bradley. Dot is horrified when Max makes a shock announcement. Bianca is mystified by an unexpected gift

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: The Millers are thrown when Mickey tells them he's leaving Walford for good. Shirley ropes Heather into her latest money-making scheme

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Peggy and Phil's unexpected return from holiday causes problems for Shirley and Vinnie. Stacey and Bradley make a shocking discovery about Sean

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ronnie's attempts to find Roxy lead her to Jack, and Stacey tries to get Sean away from the Square

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The natural history documentary follows the work of tough Australian Chris 'Brolga' Barnes, who has dedicated his life to rescuing and raising orphaned kangaroos in the outback near Alice Springs. This two-part film follows Brolga as he expands operations by opening his doors to a whole new bunch of animals, including a trio of camels, a southern hairy-nosed wombat and three emu chicks, as well as building a wildlife hospital

programme.title: Elephant Diaries
programme.desc: Series about a year in the life of orphaned baby elephants at the David Shelrick Wildlife Trust in Kenya. Two-year-old Wendi has taken on the role of the leader of the herd. Presented by Michaela Strachan and Jonathan Scott

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross creates impressive skies, misty waters and snowy mountains - completed by Bob using just his specially designed painting knife

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and art experts Philip Mould and Bendor Grosvenor come to the aid of a man who has inherited three paintings that he believes were painted by LS Lowry. However, he cannot prove whether they are the genuine article, and since Lowry is the British artist whose work is most frequently forged, it will take a great deal of evidence to convince the art establishment of their value. The search for the truth leads to a discovery that challenges established beliefs about the artist's life

programme.title: The Secret History of Writing
programme.desc: The history of the written word, first exploring the hunt for the origin of writing, which reveals links between ancient Egyptian hieroglyphs and modern-day letters

programme.title: The Real Doctor Zhivago
programme.desc: Stephen Smith traces the revolutionary beginnings of Boris Pasternak's Doctor Zhivago, a novel that took the author 20 years to write while knowing it could result in his death

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Immersive viewing experience, featuring a combination of sequences from natural history programmes with narration from Buddhist monk Andy Puddicombe

programme.title: Sold! Inside the World's Biggest Auction House
programme.desc: First of a two-part documentary going behind the scenes at Christie's to chart a year in the life of the international institution, recording the highs and lows of life in the art business, from London to Dubai, via Hong Kong and New York

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross creates impressive skies, misty waters and snowy mountains - completed by Bob using just his specially designed painting knife

programme.title: Elephant Diaries

programme.title: The Secret History of Writing
programme.desc: The history of the written word, first exploring the hunt for the origin of writing, which reveals links between ancient Egyptian hieroglyphs and modern-day letters

programme.title: Elephant Diaries
programme.desc: Jonathan Scott watches the older orphans being introduced to a herd of wild elephants, and meets a formidable matriarch. Michaela Strachan helps break up the nursery herd in preparation for their release, but the move is disrupted when one of the animals panics

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates a tiny waterfall tucked beside a glassy lake, surrounded by snowy majestic peaks and distant evergreens

programme.title: Earth's Great Rivers
programme.desc: Documentary following three of the world's largest rivers, beginning with the Amazon, uncovering some of the latest discoveries, including the 'Blue Lagoon' which is home to a newly discovered species of cichlid. The programme also explores some of the Amazon's flooded forests, home to an array of unusual animals, and cameras follow giant river otters on a fishing foray on the rocky terraces of the Cristalino river

programme.title: Australia with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer embarks on a journey across the country, meeting a diverse array of people and encountering exotic wildlife. He begins in Northern Territory, where he discovers how the camel is threatening the fragile desert ecosystem, then heads through wine country to the South Australian city of Port Lincoln to dive with critically endangered southern blue fin tuna. In Western Australia, he joins a 21st-century gold rush and ends his first leg in state capital Perth, where he meets a former binman from Hull who now has a house in the sun, a private pool and his own boat

programme.title: Chateau Chunder: When Australian Wine Changed the World
programme.desc: Documentary examining how winemakers in Australia redefined their industry, turning it into one of the world's leading exponents of making and marketing the beverage. Wine critics including Oz Clarke and Jancis Robinson reveal that good value, consistent quality and simple classification were key in helping to improve the reputation and popularity of the export

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe shows what can be learned from the ways in which animals adapt to changes in the world around them

programme.desc: A decapitated corpse and drug-running bring taciturn detective Jay Swan to the remote, tight-lipped coastal community of Gideon

programme.desc: In this episode Dr. Maggie reveals the gamechangers of space – missions and spacecraft from the Space Shuttle to the Hubble Space Telescope that have transformed our lives on earth and beyond. Dr. Maggie’s Star Guest is NASA astronaut Kathy Sullivan who’s a gamechanger herself. She flew in the space shuttle three times, she delivered Hubble into orbit and was the first American woman to walk in space! Plus, Dr Maggie counts down the Top 5 Most Expensive Missions and all your space questions are answered in Ask Maggie. Buckle up, it’s time to go Out Of This World!

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: The lives of two young brothers living on the Island of Fofoa in Tonga. After living through the worst tropical cyclone in memory, Felix and Luca enlist a local conservation charity to help them survey their coral reef to see the effects of the storm and check the long term damage from climate change. Protecting the marine life is vital to keep attracting the migrating whales back every year, not least because the boys' favourite hobby is taking visitors swimming to meet these magnificent animals

programme.title: Matilda and the Ramsay Bunch
programme.desc: Matilda and the Ramsay Bunch are back home in London and they're throwing a party for some of their friends. With a party to organise the gang get to work - Matilda's brother Jack and sisters Megan and Holly take shopping to another level as they speed along the river Thames on a high-performance rib to pick up supplies. Matilda's in charge of the party food: build your own spicy sausage pitta bread sandwiches with zingy yoghurt sauce and popcorn cakes. The popcorn cakes idea came from Scarlet, a Matilda and the Ramsay Bunch viewer who emailed it to Matilda's Food Blog. Great food, friends and fun, and the party finishing with a bang when the guys play an explosive game with a watermelon - parties don't get much better!

programme.desc: As Matilda and her family set off on their exciting climb over the O2 London arena, they look back over the fantastic summer they've just had, remembering everything they've done, the people they've met, the places they've been and the delicious food Matilda has cooked

But this young boy with big dreams doesn't let anything get in the way of his ambition. programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: My Dog Ears follows the journey of 12-year-old Joe who is hoping to receive a very precious gift - a dog that will become his ears. Joe has been profoundly deaf since he was a baby and a specially trained hearing dog could bring him the independence and confidence he so craves. Only 30 children in the UK have one of these dogs and Joe is next on the waiting list, but his journey isn't straightforward. Joe also has a Titanium leg and needs an operation to help him walk before he can receive his dog. Can Joe fully recover from his surgery and complete the training that will allow him to have a new best friend - his very own hearing dog?

programme.title: Endlings
programme.desc: Set 20 years in the future, Endlings is the empowering story of four teenagers in foster care who discover they're not alone in the universe, even though sometimes it can feel like they are. Halfway across the world, Tuko is alone. After the death of her mate, Tuko becomes the last elephant in existence--what biologists call an endling. Her endling status summons to Earth a mysterious Alien who is on an intergalactic quest to save the last member of near-extinct species. After Tuko is collected, the Alien's ship malfunctions and sends it on a crash course with the farm where Julia (Kamaia Fairburn), Johnny (Edison Grant), Tabby (Michela Luci) and Finn (Cale Thomas Ferrin) live with their foster dad, Mr. Leopold (Neil Crone). Before crashing, the Alien releases all of its cosmic collection into the fields and wilderness surrounding the property. This kicks off a high-action life-altering mission to retrieve these fantastical creatures before the local police, Tuko's caretaker (Oyin Olad

programme.desc: Delivery Duckling arrives and tells Love Monster he has been chosen to be sipping host at a special Hot Chocolate Day ceremony. She then cycles off before telling him what a sipping host does. Love Monster goes to Elder Panda to ask his advice, and he tells him there are many ways to serve hot chocolate - each person needs to find their own unique way. Elder Panda adds honey and banana from his garden to his. Love Monster starts the ceremony by adding his signature marshmallows, but then Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny announces she puts marshmallows in hers too. Love Monster thinks he needs to use something more unusual, so he adds pickles, but this doesn’t go down well - in fact, it tastes horrible! It turns out the reason why he was chosen was so that he could make hot chocolate the way he always makes it. He does, and everyone loves it.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster has been chosen as the prize-picker at the Harvest Day contest, but when he gets there it turns out there is only one prize and three teams. This means he has to choose a winner between his friends – Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, Bad Idea Puppy and the In-Chicks. Round one is fancy fashion foodies, and everyone loves Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, so Love Monster thinks he can just pick her. Then it is round two – the veggie variety show. Everyone loves the In-Chicks, so Love Monster now has to choose between them and Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny. Then it is the final round, which is the tantalizing taste test of home grown goodies, and this time everyone loves Bad Idea Puppy. Love Monster has no idea how he can pick a winner and runs off and hides, but then he realises Bad Idea Puppy always makes him laugh, so he declares him the winner.

programme.desc: O goes through the magnifying glass to where the grass is blue, the trees are square and nonsense words make friendly monsters

programme.title: Tee and Mo

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: The potatoes sing a guitar-driven rock 'n' roll anthem celebrating their feelings

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: The lamb is paired with a sneezy Stripey for a boat-making project, and finds his partner's problem comes in handy for pushing their paper-hat creation to the finish line

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Dog and Pug visit King Arthur in his castle.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The bunny accidentally spills Krispos all over the kitchen floor when driving his car around the breakfast table - so tries to clean them up with a 'voo-voo cleaner'

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The tea-loving bird-like characters see a man take his chair out into the garden, and decide to bring some more things outside. Meanwhile, Peekaboo gets stuck outside the boat

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friend pretend to be superheroes for the day. Meanwhile, Super Tumble goes to the rescue of Shopkeeper Tumble, Baker Tumble and Aunt Polly

programme.title: Teletubbies

programme.title: Hey Duggee

programme.title: Andy's Dinosaur Toybox

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: A little boy journeys from real-life into the animated art world he creates, where his friends help him handle situations that make him feel anxious

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Four-year-old twin brothers Josh and Zac, and their five-year-old brother Nathan see who can draw the biggest chalk dragon on the garden patio. Then it's pizza for dinner and the three brothers become pizza chefs! They add lots of toppings to their pizza's, but how will they taste?

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: Animation bringing to life the African fable of how a wildebeest stampede gave the crocodile its distinctive bumpy back

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Naomi makes a very special Senegalese seafood dish called Mafe Fish. She serves it with white fluffy rice and a traditional, colourful Senegalese drink

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: It's the day after the Brooklyn-Bells' wedding and time to say goodbye to the puppy

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: A new go-kart appears in the playground and all the youngsters want to have a go on it

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: Katie and Liam are looking for a large piece of paper to do their next big painting on, but when their parents are too busy in the Post Office to help and Grannie Island is unable to assist, the feisty seven-year-old calls at the Lady Artist's house in the hope she will know where to get one from

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Sarah and Duck take an unexpected trip whilst on their way to buy some fudge

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Mo is very excited by the arrival of a new lamp, but Tee is more interested in the box it came in. Together, the pair find the perfect place to put the lamp, using the box and a little bit of imagination

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Q joins U to make some noise and create a vast array of new words

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say 'dog', ahead of a whirlwind word adventure. The show explores how dogs look and what they do with a tiny dog, a fluffy dog and a spotty dog surprise

programme.title: Numberblocks

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The friends play on a new climbing frame, but Bing waits and watches Padno go down a slide because he is too scared to try

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends go on a treasure hunt, and Mr Tumble's cake goes on an adventure!

programme.title: Teletubbies

programme.title: Tinpo
programme.desc: Rain stops play when Bo, Jo and Mo want a game of Odd Ball, and Logi-Po is out of his depth looking after the three Kid Po

programme.title: Olobob Top

programme.desc: Lily and Felix explain to Hugo all the different ways people are kind to one another, before Hugo gives almost all of his balloons away to his friends

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Gem and her teams of Swashbucklers try to win her jewels back from pirates Cook, Line and Sinker. Game show for children

programme.title: Hey Duggee
