programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Benjamin gets a pet snail, but his friends cannot see the appeal, especially when it attracts the attention of Old Brown

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster has been chosen as the prize-picker at the Harvest Day contest, but when he gets there it turns out there is only one prize and three teams. This means he has to choose a winner between his friends – Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, Bad Idea Puppy and the In-Chicks. Round one is fancy fashion foodies, and everyone loves Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, so Love Monster thinks he can just pick her. Then it is round two – the veggie variety show. Everyone loves the In-Chicks, so Love Monster now has to choose between them and Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny. Then it is the final round, which is the tantalizing taste test of home grown goodies, and this time everyone loves Bad Idea Puppy. Love Monster has no idea how he can pick a winner and runs off and hides, but then he realises Bad Idea Puppy always makes him laugh, so he declares him the winner.

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie Moate goes out and about exploring different ways to travel. She visits an airfield and uses a special camera to find out how the rotor works to help make a helicopter fly. An animation shows how changing the angle of the rotor blades makes the helicopter go forwards and backwards. Next, Maddie visits a workshop to see how a tricycle is constructed from a big box of parts and to see how the tricycle can be adapted to suit a user's needs

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: When Ranger Bevel gets stuck in a pile of soft snow on High Snowy Mountain, Bitz needs to work out how they can pull him out without sinking into it too

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: A trip to see fireflies becomes a rescue when Xuli and Glitch get stuck in a swamp.

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: Tim goes to the vet's with Dad to see Grandma, who has been waiting there all day with her dog Mossy. The vet does some tests to find out what is wrong with her

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Animated fun for younger viewers featuring tales of the world's worst puppy who woofs and wags his way into the lives of an unassuming family

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo & Gran Gran have their own sports day, including an egg and spoon race.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Umbrella needs Sarah and Duck's help after he promises to put on a magic show

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Inside the little toy house, Colly Wobble discovers a toy suitcase

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: A strange little egg-shaped creature lands on the planet and proceeds to explore, but it gets stuck in a cave deep underground, where it is heard by the sleeping Clangers. Michael Palin narrates

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Pinky Ponk rocks back and forth while stuck up a tree, eventually flying off and taking everybody to the dance

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Rosamund Pike reads Never Ask a Dinosaur to Dinner, by Gareth Edwards and Guy Parker-Rees

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Martin Geissler and Tina Daheley present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Arsenal v West Ham United at Emirates Stadium and Manchester United v Crystal Palace at Old Trafford. The Gunners and Red Devls both had high hopes of strong campaigns after each ending their domestic campaigns on a high last time out, and will have expected home wins. Plus, Everton v West Bromwich Albion and Leeds United v Fulham

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, and reports from around Wales

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Mark and Gill Johnson fly out to Perth for a trial week to check out the property, work and lifestyle they could have if they moved there permanently. Aged in their forties, the pair know it's either now or never, but as an only child Mark has split loyalties. Nicki Chapman follows their progress in reaching a decision

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams search for items at Wetherby Racecourse in West Yorkshire, while Charlie Ross challenges fairgoers to guess his mystery item

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley marks Harvest in Saddleworth, on the Yorkshire-Lancashire border, and Katie Piper meets a Christian minister helping Welsh farmers through times of crisis

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Lucy Alexander explore a detached house in Chesterton, Staffordshire, a former police office in Netherton in the Midlands and a Victorian house in Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore and her team revamp a pair of brown atomic chairs with the help of upholsterer Anthony Devine, and later find a mid-century hall unit that needs bringing up to date

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Documentary following 26-year-old Teleri Fielden, who won a scholarship to run National Trust farm Llyndy Isaf near Beddgelert in north Wales for a year. Teleri is determined to break into the male-dominated industry, but will she be able to cope with lambing on one of the toughest farms in Snowdonia during one of the bleakest springs in living memory? Part of the Our Lives documentary strand

programme.title: A Special School
programme.desc: Observational documentary following the reality of life in Britain's biggest special school. In the first episode pupils face their fears at an outdoor adventure centre. The focus of the trip is for the young people to have an opportunity to challenge their disability physically, and abseiling in a wheelchair epitomises this experience

programme.title: Hidden Wales with Will Millard
programme.desc: Wales once led the world in the production of coal, exporting the black gold to the four corners of the Earth. Since that industry collapsed much of the infrastructure has been destroyed, forgotten or hidden away. Here, the adventurer goes in search of those final pieces of coal history, visiting one of the country's last coal mines, where they have dug up centuries' old workings. Will also explores the abandoned buildings of some south Wales forgotten collieries and lands on an island that was responsible for wrecking hundreds of coal ships

programme.title: Coal House
programme.desc: The children learn how to cope with 1920s schooling and discipline, while the mothers get to grips with mangles, ranges and the first tin bath as the families continue settling into the punishing routine of a 1927 coal mining community. Nicola Reynolds narrates

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith are with four of the UK's leading shepherds and their sheepdogs for this year's Countryfile One Man and His Dog competition, which takes place on a tough course designed to test man and dog in the grounds of historic Hardwick Hall in Derbyshire. The coronavirus means things have to look a little different this year, with just one competitor from each home nation vying for glory. But this socially-distanced shepherding showdown is as testing and competitive as ever. The premier league of shepherding talent from England, Wales, Ireland and Scotland must battle it out over two rounds - the 'singles', where they run one dog, and the notoriously difficult 'brace', where they must work two dogs at the same time. Whoever gets the highest combined score will secure the pride of their nation and the coveted title

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Leicester City v Burnley (Kick-off 7.00pm). All the action from the Premier League encounter at King Power Stadium. Leicester finished in a more than credible fifth place last season, but will have been disappointed to miss out on the Champions League places as their form dipped once the league restarted. In contrast, Burnley started the campaign slowly, but finished it strongly to end the season in 10th and their defensive organisation is likely to provide the Foxes with a difficult test this evening

programme.title: Us
programme.desc: Douglas and Connie Petersen look forward to a holiday of a lifetime, touring Europe with their teenage son Albie before he leaves home for university. However, the trip soon becomes a desperate quest to save the couple's marriage. The holiday begins in Paris, where Douglas and Connie struggle to hide the tension between them from Albie, who's already less-than-thrilled to be backpacking with his parents. On the family's first night abroad, Albie exchanges an evening spent with his mum and dad for time alone on the streets of Paris with his guitar. Drama based on the novel by David Nicholls. Starring Tom Hollander, Saskia Reeves and Tom Taylor

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Chelsea v Liverpool and Southampton v Tottenham Hotspur. The Blues were looking to make a statement against champions Liverpool after spending heavily in the summer, while the Saints were hoping to emulate their shock 1-0 win at home to Spurs last term. Plus, Leicester City v Burnley, Newcastle v Brighton & Hove Albion and a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: Squad Goals
programme.desc: With a big game looming against Manchester United. West Ham's leading scorer Martha Thomas is still suffering from an injury, and seeks emotional support from her girlfriend Ellie

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: With Super Bowl winner Osi Umenyiora, world champion swimmer Keri-anne Payne, former England defender Stuart Pearce and ex-World Superbike champion Neil Hodgson. Presented by Sue Barker, with team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley ask for viewers' help with unsolved cases, and reporter John-Paul Davies is out and about with stories from across the UK

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell meet developers who all have very ambitious plans for their auction buys in Littleover in Derby, Barking in Essex and Congleton in Cheshire. Ideas range from a two-floor extension, creating an extra home and a radical rethink on the use of one of the buildings

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: A modern slavery unit in north Wales tries to help suspected sex workers in a caravan, while the criminal owners of a light aircraft are forced to put the item up for sale

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: In a special programme, Eric Knowles revisits objects that remind people of good times, and looks at how antiques and music can help those living with memory loss and dementia

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Drama set during the Second World War, following the lives and loves of a group of women who decide to serve their country by joining the Women's Land Army. Ellen is confronted by a face from the past when Hoxley Manor is transformed into a military hospital, Connie and Henry plan their engagement party, and Joyce receives news about John. A new arrival at Pasture Farm befriends Iris, and Finch makes a lucrative discovery

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers forage for food, exploring Britain's fields and hedgerows in search of free ingredients that will form the basis of spectacular dishes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a recently married couple - who are moving from Australia - find a new home in Derbyshire, on a budget of £750,000

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie, Harry and David visit Ibstock, Leicestershire, where BBQ fan Norman needs a place to grill and chill, and baking-mad Piper wants plants to add flavour to her cakes

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The experts take on the restoration of a tired teddy bear, an equestrian ceremonial blanket and a portable police crime office

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Richard Bilton uncovers secret reports that expose how banks have failed to tackle crime and how terrorists, money launderers and crime bosses are able to use the same banks as ordinary citizens. He also reveals the double life of the man who funded the Brexit Party, secret deals at the top of British football and the business deals billionaires would rather you didn't know about

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As Gray is questioned by the police, Karen, Mitch and the rest of the family return from Southend to learn the awful truth of what has happened. Ruby is forced to come clean to Martin that someone has been stealing from her bank account, and with Ben in need of legal assistance, Phil confronts Callum, asking if he had known his boyfriend's plan all along

programme.title: A Special School
programme.desc: Rebecca Jones introduces her class of eight competitive, autistic boys, each with their own individual needs, including Ethan - who likes to answer the school's telephone. Fifteen-year-old Luke was born with schizencephaly - a hole in his brain - which has left him with long-term physical health problems. Ysgol y Deri is his lifeline and with voice-generating software, teachers are helping him to express his emotions

programme.title: The 7.39
programme.desc: Carl Matthews is happily married with two teenage kids - but he also feels stuck in a rut. As he waits on the platform for the daily commute into Waterloo, he realises his life has become predictable. Sally Thorn has recently moved out of London, where she still works as the manager of a West End health club. But she's unsure if she likes her new suburban life - or the prospect of marriage to personal trainer Ryan. So when a fight over a seat on the 7.39 train leads Carl and Sally to start talking, there's a spark between the pair - and suddenly their daily journeys become a whole lot more interesting. David Morrissey and Sheridan Smith star in this two-part romantic drama, with Olivia Colman and Sean Maguire. Written by David Nicholls (One Day, Starter for Ten)

programme.title: Famalam
programme.desc: Fat Sam goes in search of romance, while Shawn and Leslie get married. Movie star Babatunde Warrington shoots a music video

programme.title: Yung Filly's Celeb Lock-In
programme.desc: Jamie Laing and Sophie Habboo join Yung Filly and four teams from the West Midlands. Contains some strong language

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Writer and broadcaster Victoria Coren Mitchell assumes the role of host as panellists poke fun at the week's events. Comedian Ahir Shah and Labour MP Jess Phillips join regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Appeals for information from the public about crimes around the country. Presented by Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin explore a flood-damaged house in Carlisle, a piece of land in Breadsall Hilltop in Derby, and a semi-detached house with a great view in Alsagers Bank, Newcastle-under-Lyme, then talk to the buyers about their plans for the buildings

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: In the City of London, suspected telephone fraudsters are targeted in early morning raids, while in Liverpool, a drugs gang's range rover is auctioned in a proceeds of crime sale

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: The hunt is on for a ruthless fantasist who has defrauded the NHS of thousands, and a cold-hearted insurance cheat exploiting the horror of the Manchester terror bombing

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from Ardingly Antiques Fair, West Sussex, where experts Tim Weeks and Charles Hanson assist the reds and blues as they vie, hoping to make the greater profit at auction. Away from the contest, Charlie learns about one of the bravest British Heroines of the 20th century

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Iris has a near-fatal miss on Vernon's farm, prompting Connie to issue a challenge. Finch has to protect his hidden treasure, Ellen receives a tempting offer from a visiting local dignitary, and Esther tries to hide her secret from the local gossips

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers create classic puddings and explore the history and traditions behind Britain's favourite desserts. They also take part in the world black-pudding throwing competition in Lancashire

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a dog-loving couple living in Oxford to find a new home in Carmarthenshire, and samples a regional delicacy at Britain's only manufacturer of pickled cockles

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie, Harry and David come up with plans to transform a concrete garden in Malvern with no plants to give it an Indonesian theme, spending £3000 to create a tropical jungle

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team takes on a saxophone that hasn't made a sound in over 40 years, a tarnished 80-year-old brass kettle and a shaving mirror from the trenches of the First World War

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Stacey's arrival angers Ruby and Martin, although she reassures them she is only passing through. Ruby, however, has other ideas. Kheerat tells Mitch what Chantelle had asked of him and how she was trying to leave Gray. Ben struggles under police questioning as his story cannot be verified and Sharon grows tired of Ian treating her like glorified bar staff. Denise works out Amy has a crush on Isaac and tells him he cannot tutor Amy any more

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Part one of two. Enforcement agents Ben Dirom and Myles Whitworth visit a car dealership to get a refund for a customer who bought an unroadworthy car, but are subjected to a barrage of abuse. In Peckham, sheriffs Luke Peacock and Grant Bailey shut down a cosmetics retailer where the owner has failed to pay his debts

programme.title: The 7.39
programme.desc: Having become close friends on their daily commute, Carl and Sally are forced to fight their feelings for one another, fully aware that the repercussions of a full-blown affair would be life-changing for both of them. And yet despite their best intentions, it seems they simply can't help falling in love. David Morrissey and Sheridan Smith star in the conclusion of the romantic drama, with Olivia Colman and Sean Maguire. Written by David Nicholls (One Day, Starter for Ten)

programme.title: Hell on Wheels
programme.desc: The team arrives in Finland for the European Cup. Last year, they came third, but Kid is determined to do even better and end her roller derby career on a high. However, they may have a problem in achieving this goal - the most experienced blocker has misplaced her passport and is stuck in Cardiff. They also face a tournament format as unforgiving as the sport itself, where one defeat can cost a team everything

programme.title: Just One Night
programme.desc: Poppy and Elliot put their trust and compatibility to the test by dating someone new

programme.title: Just One Night
programme.desc: Kareem and Jahz dip their feet in the dating waters to try to fix their relationship issues

programme.title: Peter Kay's Stand-Up Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: The stand-up comedian relives his finest career moments from the last 25 years, taking in his stage shows, theatre work and hit sitcoms including Phoenix Nights and Car Share

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Filmed in the autumn of 2019, cameras follow a new cast of characters from the London Ambulance Service as they care for the nine million people of the city. In the first episode, ambulance crew members Kate and Femi are dispatched to a young man who is struggling to breathe during a severe anxiety attack. Meanwhile, just three hours into the morning shift, control deal with the third assault of the day, having already responded to four stabbings

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple looking for a home in Oxfordshire on a budget of £1.5 million, and also braves the county's waters during an exhilarating attempt at punt racing

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore looks for items to upcycle at Wallyford Recycling Centre in Scotland, where she comes across a broken dining table, three fabric chairs, and a collection of lawn bowls

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Cwmcarn, south Wales, Kim and Simon's steep garden is little more than three huge, muddy tiers, so Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich set out to turn it into a relaxing space worthy of the area's amazing views

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith are with four of the UK's leading shepherds and their sheepdogs for this year's Countryfile One Man and His Dog competition, which takes place on a tough course designed to test man and dog in the grounds of historic Hardwick Hall in Derbyshire. The coronavirus means things have to look a little different this year, with just one competitor from each home nation vying for glory. But this socially-distanced shepherding showdown is as testing and competitive as ever. The premier league of shepherding talent from England, Wales, Ireland and Scotland must battle it out over two rounds - the 'singles', where they run one dog, and the notoriously difficult 'brace', where they must work two dogs at the same time. Whoever gets the highest combined score will secure the pride of their nation and the coveted title

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Leicester City v Burnley (Kick-off 7.00pm). All the action from the Premier League encounter at King Power Stadium. Leicester finished in a more than credible fifth place last season, but will have been disappointed to miss out on the Champions League places as their form dipped once the league restarted. In contrast, Burnley started the campaign slowly, but finished it strongly to end the season in 10th and their defensive organisation is likely to provide the Foxes with a difficult test this evening

programme.title: Us
programme.desc: Douglas and Connie Petersen look forward to a holiday of a lifetime, touring Europe with their teenage son Albie before he leaves home for university. However, the trip soon becomes a desperate quest to save the couple's marriage. The holiday begins in Paris, where Douglas and Connie struggle to hide the tension between them from Albie, who's already less-than-thrilled to be backpacking with his parents. On the family's first night abroad, Albie exchanges an evening spent with his mum and dad for time alone on the streets of Paris with his guitar. Drama based on the novel by David Nicholls. Starring Tom Hollander, Saskia Reeves and Tom Taylor

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Chelsea v Liverpool and Southampton v Tottenham Hotspur. The Blues were looking to make a statement against champions Liverpool after spending heavily in the summer, while the Saints were hoping to emulate their shock 1-0 win at home to Spurs last term. Plus, Leicester City v Burnley, Newcastle v Brighton & Hove Albion and a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Steven Thompson presents action from the latest Premiership fixtures - Hibernian v Rangers at Easter Road and Aberdeen v Motherwell at Pittodrie. With analysis from Shaun Maloney and Michael Stewart

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As Gray is questioned by the police, Karen, Mitch and the rest of the family return from Southend to learn the awful truth of what has happened. Ruby is forced to come clean to Martin that someone has been stealing from her bank account, and with Ben in need of legal assistance, Phil confronts Callum, asking if he had known his boyfriend's plan all along

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Return of the comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby. Alison and Mike are trying to find a semi-harmonious routine with the ghostly housemates of Button House. The ghosts have found a way to make their new situation work for them, while the couple are working toward a new money-making venture to fund their perpetual renovations. But after a paranormal photo of the house goes viral, Alison and Mike spot an opportunity to bring money in more quickly by using the ghosts' 'talents' to create an authentic haunted-house experience

programme.title: The 7.39
programme.desc: Carl Matthews is happily married with two teenage kids - but he also feels stuck in a rut. As he waits on the platform for the daily commute into Waterloo, he realises his life has become predictable. Sally Thorn has recently moved out of London, where she still works as the manager of a West End health club. But she's unsure if she likes her new suburban life - or the prospect of marriage to personal trainer Ryan. So when a fight over a seat on the 7.39 train leads Carl and Sally to start talking, there's a spark between the pair - and suddenly their daily journeys become a whole lot more interesting. David Morrissey and Sheridan Smith star in this two-part romantic drama, with Olivia Colman and Sean Maguire. Written by David Nicholls (One Day, Starter for Ten)

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Stacey's arrival angers Ruby and Martin, although she reassures them she is only passing through. Ruby, however, has other ideas. Kheerat tells Mitch what Chantelle had asked of him and how she was trying to leave Gray. Ben struggles under police questioning as his story cannot be verified and Sharon grows tired of Ian treating her like glorified bar staff. programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Part one of two. Enforcement agents Ben Dirom and Myles Whitworth visit a car dealership to get a refund for a customer who bought an unroadworthy car, but are subjected to a barrage of abuse. In Peckham, sheriffs Luke Peacock and Grant Bailey shut down a cosmetics retailer where the owner has failed to pay his debts

programme.title: The 7.39
programme.desc: Having become close friends on their daily commute, Carl and Sally are forced to fight their feelings for one another, fully aware that the repercussions of a full-blown affair would be life-changing for both of them. And yet despite their best intentions, it seems they simply can't help falling in love. David Morrissey and Sheridan Smith star in the conclusion of the romantic drama, with Olivia Colman and Sean Maguire. Written by David Nicholls (One Day, Starter for Ten)

programme.title: Just One Night
programme.desc: Poppy and Elliot put their trust and compatibility to the test by dating someone new

programme.title: Just One Night
programme.desc: Kareem and Jahz dip their feet in the dating waters to try to fix their relationship issues

programme.title: Peter Kay's Stand-Up Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: The stand-up comedian relives his finest career moments from the last 25 years, taking in his stage shows, theatre work and hit sitcoms including Phoenix Nights and Car Share

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Filmed in the autumn of 2019, cameras follow a new cast of characters from the London Ambulance Service as they care for the nine million people of the city. In the first episode, ambulance crew members Kate and Femi are dispatched to a young man who is struggling to breathe during a severe anxiety attack. Meanwhile, just three hours into the morning shift, control deal with the third assault of the day, having already responded to four stabbings

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Martin Geissler and Tina Daheley present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Arsenal v West Ham United at Emirates Stadium and Manchester United v Crystal Palace at Old Trafford. The Gunners and Red Devls both had high hopes of strong campaigns after each ending their domestic campaigns on a high last time out, and will have expected home wins. Plus, Everton v West Bromwich Albion and Leeds United v Fulham

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: Andrew Marr is joined by Sir Keir Starmer MP, leader of the labour party, Matt Hancock MP, health and social care secretary and Bob Woodward, journalist and author of 'Rage'. With News review by Katy Balls, deputy political editor of The Spectator and Jo Coburn, Politics Live presenter

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: The political developments of the week, with policy-makers questioned on the key issues

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Mark and Gill Johnson fly out to Perth for a trial week to check out the property, work and lifestyle they could have if they moved there permanently. Aged in their forties, the pair know it's either now or never, but as an only child Mark has split loyalties. Nicki Chapman follows their progress in reaching a decision

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams search for items at Wetherby Racecourse in West Yorkshire, while Charlie Ross challenges fairgoers to guess his mystery item

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley marks Harvest in Saddleworth, on the Yorkshire-Lancashire border, and Katie Piper meets a Christian minister helping Welsh farmers through times of crisis

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Lucy Alexander explore a detached house in Chesterton, Staffordshire, a former police office in Netherton in the Midlands and a Victorian house in Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple looking for a home in Oxfordshire on a budget of £1.5 million, and also braves the county's waters during an exhilarating attempt at punt racing

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore looks for items to upcycle at Wallyford Recycling Centre in Scotland, where she comes across a broken dining table, three fabric chairs, and a collection of lawn bowls

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Cwmcarn, south Wales, Kim and Simon's steep garden is little more than three huge, muddy tiers, so Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich set out to turn it into a relaxing space worthy of the area's amazing views

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith are with four of the UK's leading shepherds and their sheepdogs for this year's Countryfile One Man and His Dog competition, which takes place on a tough course designed to test man and dog in the grounds of historic Hardwick Hall in Derbyshire. The coronavirus means things have to look a little different this year, with just one competitor from each home nation vying for glory. But this socially-distanced shepherding showdown is as testing and competitive as ever. The premier league of shepherding talent from England, Wales, Ireland and Scotland must battle it out over two rounds - the 'singles', where they run one dog, and the notoriously difficult 'brace', where they must work two dogs at the same time. Whoever gets the highest combined score will secure the pride of their nation and the coveted title

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: Documentary looking at the successful careers of some of Northern Ireland's world boxing champions, including Ryan Burnett, Brian Magee and Eamonn Magee, and how they made the tough transition into retirement. programme.title: This Sporting Life
programme.desc: In 1972 Mary Peters won the Olympic gold medal in the women's pentathlon in the Munich Games, becoming a hero in Northern Ireland and beyond. She reveals to Stephen Watson the story behind her win, how she defied threats and why her last medal win in 1974 was particularly poignant

programme.desc: Highlights of the week from the USA

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: What is the point of the world's nuclear watchdog the International Atomic Energy Agency? Its task is to ensure that countries intent on developing nuclear power don't use their programmes as cover for development of weapons of mass destruction. But is the task impossible? HARDtalk's Stephen Sackur speaks to the new IAEA chief, Rafael Grossi. From the continued bitter arguments over Iran, to North Korea, and Saudi Arabia, is the IAEA another example of a global agency undermined by geopolitical division?

programme.title: BOOKtalk

programme.title: An Drochaid (The Bridge Rising)
programme.desc: The campaign against the tolls on the Skye bridge pitted plucky Scottish islanders against the might of the Government and the Bank of America, over the building of a privately funded toll bridge, which became the only way on or off the island. This film tells an untold, bittersweet story of passion, legal challenge and financial wrangling through the testimony of some of those who took part

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz beams himself into Weasel's brain in order to take out Magoo with tranquilliser darts. Magoo loses Mr Cat and enlists his neighbour to find him.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he's in a triathlon and takes Fizz's superhuman strength headband, which Fizz needs to beat the President at a thumb war

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Today's the big day, and Magoo believes he is in a recording studio to make a hit single for famous producer, Phil Dustspeck. But is that really true?

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Tracy brothers Scott and Virgil try to find a way to shut down a dangerous device, which is capable of harnessing energy from the Aurora Borealis. An updated remake of the classic 1960s marionette adventure series, featuring the voice of Thomas Brodie-Sangster

programme.title: Dare Master
programme.desc: Danny's in Blackpool but is he brave enough to walk to the top of the UK's tallest rollercoaster?

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The popular halfwit tries to be romantic, but his idea of what that means proves to be very different to Irma's. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Jeff Banks, Michelle Ackerley, Andy Crane and Brian Conley pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: It's a family affair this week as Martin introduces a performance from his daughter Harley Moon Kemp. He and Roman also chat to singer Roachford, and author and columnist Caitlin Moran and comedian Russell Kane

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Poet Pam Ayres and comedian and health campaigner Ruby Wax join the presenter for his celebration of the great British countryside. Alan also meets a master stone carver and some rare breed miniature horses, and samples the best of British cheese. Plus, more from the organic halal farming family the Radwans

programme.title: All Around Britain
programme.desc: Ranvir Singh and Alex Beresford climb aboard Nelson's famous flagship HMS Victory at Portsmouth's historic dockyard as they present the best of ITV regional news programmes. Stories include the Scottish island abandoned by residents 90 years ago, the Yorkshire village that hosted what has been dubbed the worst ever music festival and the girl from Northern Ireland who has become an internet star with her thousands of baby frogs

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host the third semi-final of this year's talent contest, making a return to screens following the auditions episodes broadcast in the spring. Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, David Walliams and Ashley Banjo are on the judging desk, as the acts that impressed them the most return, including the golden buzzer acts - singer Fayth Ifil, comedian and singer Jon Courtney, choir Sign Along With Us, comedian Nabil Abdulrashid, and mother and daughter singing duo, Honey and Sammy. Eight acts perform in each semi-final, with two finalists selected by the judges and the votes of viewers

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show, asking celebrity contestants Jeff Stelling, Charlotte Hawkins and Andy Whyment to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. One of them will make it through to the Super Catchphrase round, where they could win £50,000 for the charity of their choice

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Mark, Steve, Grace and Charlotte work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the supermarket-based game show in which three pairs of shoppers compete in a series of challenges to win a £3,000 prize

programme.title: James Nesbitt: A Game of Two Halves
programme.desc: The accomplished actor embarks on an epic journey to see how Soccer Aid for Unicef is helping children around the world. Since 2006, Soccer Aid has raised over £38million and James will see just how far that money goes. His mission is to take essential supplies to children living in three extreme locations - the Himalayas in Nepal, the desert surrounding the Dead Sea and a remote island in the South Pacific. For James however, the journey is one into the unknown, as he won't know where he is going on each leg of his mission until just before he leaves

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts as football star Michael Owen, Strictly Come Dancing host Tess Daly and comedian Hal Cruttenden take part in the celebrity version of the game show, in which they must answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo takes over hosting duties on this revamped version of the popular game show, which sees two families trying to guess the answers to survey-based questions posed to 100 people. And for anyone who hasn't seen it, the family who score the most points go through to the final round, where they play for the a £30,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Singapore Grip
programme.desc: Matthew Webb settles in and is introduced to the family but after a tour of his rubber plantation he is troubled to see that questionable practices are taking place. Joan sweeps him off to an amusement part with Monty and Ehrendorf, where Vera becomes intrigued by the newcomer, and soon he finds himself in the middle of a tug-of-war between Vera and Joan, battling for his affections. But very soon Singapore is hit by a war of a different kind. Adaptation of the novel by JG Farrell, starring Luke Treadaway, David Morrissey, Georgia Blizzard and Elizabeth Tan

programme.title: The Real 'Des': The Dennis Nilsen Story
programme.desc: Following on from the three-part drama about the serial killer Dennis Nilsen, this documentary explores the personality of the man behind the crimes - on the surface a mild-mannered civil servant who admitted to murdering up to 15 young men over a four-year period in the late 1980s and early 1980s, disposing of their bodies in the garden and down the drain. The film includes footage of an interview Nilsen gave to ITV at Albany Prison in 1992, contributions from victims' families and insights from criminologist David Wilson, who corresponded with Nilsen over several decades. Narrated by David Tennant

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. Border force officer Kat decides whether to allow a holiday-maker arriving with little money in and a confusing story into the UK. Meanwhile, Customer Service Agent Mr Sho is having issues with a 400-year-old Japanese tradition

programme.title: Harbour Cops
programme.desc: Documentary following the daily work of Dyfed-Powys Police officers over the busy summer months. Dyfed-Powys is the smallest police force in the UK, but has to patrol the largest police area, their jurisdiction taking in national parks and a lengthy run of the spectacular South Wales coastline. In this first episode, officers in the seaside town of Tenby on the western side of Carmarthen Bay have to deal with rowdy holidaymakers causing trouble and track down a pensioner who has been reported missing

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Richard John Neil presents from Brands Hatch, featuring round four of the Dunlop Mini Challenge and round three of the Porsche Carrera Cup GB

programme.title: Judge Rinder

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep

New quiz rounds feature alongside the legendary trolley dashes and the most famous pastel jumpers on television

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Tom, Wyn, Harriet and Heather pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Leyla steps in to help Gabby, Belle is devastated that her family have turned against her, and it remains to be seen whether Dan will reveal the truth

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack attacks Hope after Abi advises him to tackle bullies, Nicky and Daniel get closer, and Leanne is overwhelmed when Nick offers to sell his shares in Underworld to raise cash for Oliver's treatment

programme.title: Inside Animal A&E
programme.desc: In London, ambulance driver Lee picks up senior citizen and regular Blue Cross patient - 17-year-old tabby Twinkle - who has a sore mouth. Elsewhere in the capital, vet Roshene is heading up the mobile clinic and sees bulldog Ace, who has a nasty swelling and is immediately transferred to the hospital for surgery - where vet Aysha find a surprising reason for his swollen face

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: When a suspicious Debbie sees Abi meet up with Peter in the ginnel she demands to know what is going on. Tracy is worried when Steve suggests they should sell Streetcars and the florist to fund Oliver's treatment. Billy tells Mary that he's hired a private detective to hunt for Todd

programme.title: Rose West & Myra Hindley: Their Untold Story with Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: The veteran journalist tells the extraordinary untold story of how the two most notorious women in British crime, Rose West and Myra Hindley, became lesbian lovers in jail. Former Category A prisoners, including reformed Mafia member Marisa Merico and ex-gangster Linda Calvey, tell Sir Trevor how Rose West revealed her murderous temper in prison, while former prison governor Veronica Bird, police officers and prison visitors describe how Hindley cleverly manipulated people in jail, even persuading a female guard to help her escape

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Flying for Britain with David Jason
programme.desc: The actor meets members of the RAF's Battle of Britain Memorial Flight, charged with preserving historic aircraft from the Second World War. The team is emerging from lockdown, having been forced to cancel several anniversary events. A qualified pilot, David joins them in their historic Spitfires and Hurricanes for a special tribute fly-past

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the 21st and final stage of this year's race, as the riders took on a flat 122km course from Mantes-la-Jolie to the Champs-Elysees in the heart of Paris. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Parveen's Indian Kitchen
programme.desc: Parveen Ashraf visits an unusual vegetable market in Mumbai that is only foe early risers, and samples some street food and a platter made up of 44 different dishes. Back in the UK, she prepares spicy fried okra chips and mango lassi, offers tips on the best fruit and nuts, and catches up with award-winning chef Vivek Singh at his London restaurant

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Andrea tells Moira the secret, Victoria returns full of resolve, and the penny drops for Liv

programme.title: Harbour Cops
programme.desc: Public information officer Nigel Lewis is called in to deal with a cow that is on the loose and causing mayhem. Out at sea, the police elite marine unit set out to prevent a dangerous situation when a boat strays into a restricted area

programme.title: Love Your Garden Themed Specials
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh takes a look back at memorable exotic gardens created by his team, and the unusual twists the show has put on a challenge. The three projects featured in this special are a tranquil Japanese garden, a Moroccan oasis, and a theatrical Italian masterpiece

programme.title: Appropriate Adult
programme.desc: Part one of two. Drama telling the story of how serial killers Fred and Rosemary West were brought to justice. In 1994 the life of Gloucestershire voluntary worker Janet Leach is turned upside down when she is asked by police to sit in on interviews as an independent safeguard on behalf of a man they have arrested. Within minutes he makes a shocking confession of murder. From then on she is drawn into the centre of the investigation and a complex personal relationship with West, who tells her information about other victims that she cannot pass on to the authorities because of her obligation of confidentiality to him. Starring Emily Watson, Dominic West, Monica Dolan and Robert Glenister

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: The documentary explores how the Queen has responded in times of sadness and grief, as she faced personal tragedies and national disasters including the Aberfan disaster, the Grenfell fire, and the IRA's murder of Lord Mountbatten. The Queen also faced her so-called 'Annus Horribilis' in 1992, when family scandal, press ridicule and the catastrophic Windsor Castle fire tested her skills as monarch and mother to the limit, and, of course the death of Diana, Princess of Wales, when her slow response to the nation's grief threatened to destabilise the monarchy

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz beams himself into Weasel's brain in order to take out Magoo with tranquilliser darts. Magoo loses Mr Cat and enlists his neighbour to find him.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he's in a triathlon and takes Fizz's superhuman strength headband, which Fizz needs to beat the President at a thumb war

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Today's the big day, and Magoo believes he is in a recording studio to make a hit single for famous producer, Phil Dustspeck. But is that really true?

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Tracy brothers Scott and Virgil try to find a way to shut down a dangerous device, which is capable of harnessing energy from the Aurora Borealis. An updated remake of the classic 1960s marionette adventure series, featuring the voice of Thomas Brodie-Sangster

programme.title: Dare Master
programme.desc: Danny's in Blackpool but is he brave enough to walk to the top of the UK's tallest rollercoaster?

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The popular halfwit tries to be romantic, but his idea of what that means proves to be very different to Irma's. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Jeff Banks, Michelle Ackerley, Andy Crane and Brian Conley pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: It's a family affair this week as Martin introduces a performance from his daughter Harley Moon Kemp. He and Roman also chat to singer Roachford, and author and columnist Caitlin Moran and comedian Russell Kane

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Poet Pam Ayres and comedian and health campaigner Ruby Wax join the presenter for his celebration of the great British countryside. Alan also meets a master stone carver and some rare breed miniature horses, and samples the best of British cheese. Plus, more from the organic halal farming family the Radwans

programme.title: All Around Britain
programme.desc: Ranvir Singh and Alex Beresford climb aboard Nelson's famous flagship HMS Victory at Portsmouth's historic dockyard as they present the best of ITV regional news programmes. Stories include the Scottish island abandoned by residents 90 years ago, the Yorkshire village that hosted what has been dubbed the worst ever music festival and the girl from Northern Ireland who has become an internet star with her thousands of baby frogs

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host the third semi-final of this year's talent contest, making a return to screens following the auditions episodes broadcast in the spring. Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, David Walliams and Ashley Banjo are on the judging desk, as the acts that impressed them the most return, including the golden buzzer acts - singer Fayth Ifil, comedian and singer Jon Courtney, choir Sign Along With Us, comedian Nabil Abdulrashid, and mother and daughter singing duo, Honey and Sammy. Eight acts perform in each semi-final, with two finalists selected by the judges and the votes of viewers

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show, asking celebrity contestants Jeff Stelling, Charlotte Hawkins and Andy Whyment to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. One of them will make it through to the Super Catchphrase round, where they could win £50,000 for the charity of their choice

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Mark, Steve, Grace and Charlotte work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show
programme.desc: The host welcomes actress Wanda Sykes to the studio, who tells her all about Visible: Out on Television. Plus, Michael Kiwanuka performs Hero from his album Kiwanuka

programme.desc: Leyla steps in to help Gabby, Belle is devastated that her family have turned against her, and it remains to be seen whether Dan will reveal the truth

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack attacks Hope after Abi advises him to tackle bullies, Nicky and Daniel get closer, and Leanne is overwhelmed when Nick offers to sell his shares in Underworld to raise cash for Oliver's treatment

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: When a suspicious Debbie sees Abi meet up with Peter in the ginnel she demands to know what is going on. Tracy is worried when Steve suggests they should sell Streetcars and the florist to fund Oliver's treatment. Billy tells Mary that he's hired a private detective to hunt for Todd

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show
programme.desc: Justin Bieber joins the host on her birthday show and Tanya Tucker and Brandi Carlile perform Hard Luck

programme.title: Ellen's Game of Games
programme.desc: Ellen DeGeneres brings her love for fun to the stage with super-sized versions of some of the most popular games from her daytime show

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: Rhino, Night Angel, Frog and Turtle perform in the semi-final. Who will impress the judges and guest Jay Pharoah, and who will be going home at this late stage?

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three suitors buy an outfit for politics student Demi, who then goes on a date with the person whose taste in clothes they liked best

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness lines up another panel of single ladies, giving them a chance to appraise the worthiness of a selection of bachelors seeking romance. Among the men entering the 'love lift' this week are fitness instructor Dathan from London, pianist Scott from Huddersfield, research executive George from Surrey, and Nick, a construction worker from New Zealand

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers on their camcorders and mobile phones, featuring a dog with a lightsaber, a scooter trick misfiring and a possessed parasol

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring performing tots, a countdown of five teenagers in pain and the familiar line-up of crazy animals, below-the-belt mishaps and practical jokes

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan offers Jenny some relationship advice, while Walden enters into a new project with a former employee. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer and Ashton Kutcher

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden feels put out when Nicole prioritises her software project over him, so he offers to help her with it. Meanwhile, Larry persuades Alan to go on a blind date. Guest-starring Jeff Probst

programme.title: Love Island USA
programme.desc: The US version of the international reality sensation returns. Arielle Vandenburg hosts

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter becomes jealous of Lois when she upstages him at a Red Sox fantasy baseball camp. Meanwhile, Stewie runs away from home when Meg is left to babysit him

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter is asked to fill in for a mall Santa, but soon gets drunk on power when he realises that he can get away with anything while dressed as Father Christmas. Meanwhile, Stewie and Brian crash holiday office parties for free food, drama and women

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Quagmire lands free plane tickets for a guys' trip to San Francisco, but his friends begin to doubt his flying abilities after getting a look at the autopilot function

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Lindsey, the Smiths' regular babysitter (guest star Alison Brie), breaks her leg during a soccer match, and Hayley volunteers to look after Steve while Stan and Francine head out on a date. However, when the nerdy teen escapes his sister's attention, he and his friends embark on a quest to gain entry to a big house party, but end up crossing paths with an obsessive meth dealer

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan is embarrassed by Steve's recreational activities, so he takes him to Mexico to teach him how to be more manly - but the plan backfires when they are kidnapped by members of a local drug cartel

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Donna accuses Cleveland of being lazy, so he tries to prove he is every bit as personally organised and active as she is - by planning a beer walk

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan offers Jenny some relationship advice, while Walden enters into a new project with a former employee. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer and Ashton Kutcher

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden feels put out when Nicole prioritises her software project over him, so he offers to help her with it. Meanwhile, Larry persuades Alan to go on a blind date. Guest-starring Jeff Probst

programme.title: Roswell, New Mexico
programme.desc: Cameron confronts Jesse about where her sister is, and Liz is on the verge of a breakthrough in her attempt to save Max - but she needs a favour from Kyle

programme.title: The Almost Impossible Gameshow
programme.desc: Action-packed game show with hilarious commentary from Irish comedy duo The Rubberbandits. 10 contestants are given 50 lives to take on a series of tricky challenges

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid has problems when Jean pleads for a new freezer

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean and Sally interpret Sid's dreams and Trevor tries to recreate the atmosphere of the previous night

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Billy reports his suspicions to the police, David hears from his house's insurers and Toyah answers tough questions concerning her past

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A professor feels humiliated when her internet dating video is leaked onto a website and watched by her students. The next morning she is found dead, the victim of what appears to be a straightforward suicide. However, Lewis is not convinced and digs deeper, identifying several suspects including the site's founder - but as he and Hathaway get closer to the truth amid a rising death toll, they find their personal and professional lives dredged up online for all to see. Guest starring Roxanne McKee (Game of Thrones) and Toby Stephens (Die Another Day), with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Carry On Cruising (1962)
programme.desc: The captain of a cruise ship bound for the Mediterranean is less than impressed when five of his regular shipmates are replaced at the last minute. Despite their best efforts to earn his respect and an eagerness to please, the new recruits seem doomed to cause chaos whatever they do. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Liz Fraser and Kenneth Connor

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby visits a new age commune, where a man has fallen to his death, or may have been pushed. The dead man turns out to have a connection to the leader's shady past - they set the commune up together as a confidence trick, but the leader came to believe in what he preaches. A further death raises greater suspicion, especially since a vast inheritance might be coming the group's way. Guest starring Stephen Moyer (True Blood)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A prestigious golf club is the setting for murder when a player is bludgeoned to death on the 13th hole. Barnaby and Jones discover gambling, illegal money-lending and assault are rife among the snobbish members, who are also dismissive of the local villagers and only allow them on the course for a few hours a week. Before long, another body turns up, making the detectives doubly determined to catch this terror of the fairways. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with guest appearances by Nicholas Le Prevost, John Standing and real-life golfer Sam Torrance

programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: The documentary explores how the Queen has responded in times of sadness and grief, as she faced personal tragedies and national disasters including the Aberfan disaster, the Grenfell fire, and the IRA's murder of Lord Mountbatten. The Queen also faced her so-called 'Annus Horribilis' in 1992, when family scandal, press ridicule and the catastrophic Windsor Castle fire tested her skills as monarch and mother to the limit, and, of course the death of Diana, Princess of Wales, when her slow response to the nation's grief threatened to destabilise the monarchy

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A dentist falls in love with his young assistant. His anxious wife, believing he has made plans to poison her and thereby remove the main obstacle to happiness, calls for Poirot's help. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. The detective receives a disturbing phone message from estranged brother Roy and heads to Harrogate to find him. Meanwhile at a murder scene, the team is introduced to new recruit Helen Morton, who quickly alienates her colleagues with her tactless style. The officer then makes matters worse when she finds evidence linking Banks to the victim's body and speculates he could be a suspect. Unaware everyone is searching for him, the DCI begins to delve into Roy's life and soon uncovers questionable business transactions and associates. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, with Caroline Catz (Doc Martin) and Andrea Lowe

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The detective visits his childhood home to break the news of Roy's death, and his parents plead with him to find out what happened. Defying Morton, Banks returns to the station in an attempt to lead the investigation into the murders of his brother and Jennifer Lewis, and both officers are convinced the key to cracking the cases lies with a woman being treated at a clinic. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Davina McCall: Life at the Extreme
programme.desc: Davina travels to the Costa Rican jungle, which is one of the wettest and most humid places on earth, but is a habitat in which animals thrive and is home to around 500,000 species. She learns about the ways in which creatures including sloths, poison dart frogs, tarantulas, tapirs, fer-de-lance vipers, common basilisks and spider monkeys have adapted to the conditions, before spending a night high up in the treetops of the rainforest - where a lightning striking directly overhead forces an abrupt change of plans

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective is summoned to a hotel by a mysterious veiled lady who tells him that her forthcoming marriage is under threat because she is being blackmailed over a love letter. Drama, starring David Suchet and Frances Barber

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is not feeling his usual self, so he goes to see the doctor

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maud urges Maureen to make peace with Bill. Alf demands Audrey cancels the health farm trip. Curly is angry to discover Raquel is trying to sort out his problems with the Dealers

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tricia gets a letter from Carl blaming her for him losing his parole. Sue calls on Ken and opens her heart to him about her husband leaving

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ned is upset that Jan knew about Biff's possible illness. Sophie admits to Zoe that she is scared of Butch. Tony gets the blackmail money back from Adlington

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak is worried about Butch as he did not come home the night before. PC Wilson assesses the damage to Zoe's living room. Chris sees Kim and Steve kissing in her bedroom

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Conclusion of a two-part storyline. A kidnap case leaves Vernon's plans for Liz and Ben's wedding in chaos, while Helen's dreams of a fresh start for her and Matt look set to be shattered. Jenny is relieved when she finally gets a medical diagnosis explaining her recent symptoms, but the news brings a difficult decision for husband Dennis, who has to choose between his wife and his police career. Starring newcomer Sophie Ward, Oliver Montgomery, Duncan Bell and Sarah Tansey

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is worried about an intruder, and wonders whether to scare the man off or call the police

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid informs Jean he has been given a temporary promotion at work, but she soon begins to doubt what he says. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Henpecked George finally finds a job, but the line of work could make him unpopular with road users. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A confrontation between hunt stewards and saboteurs leads to the suspicious death of a protester - and makes Jack wonder whether the thrill of the chase has given the horsey set a taste for human blood. Whodunit, starring David Jason as DI Frost, with Bruce Alexander, Sean Pertwee, Del Henney and Tat Whalley

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Veteran singer and keyboard player Alan Price guest stars in this festive episode of the rural drama, as the leader of a jazz band. Travelling to Aidensfield, where they've been booked by Vernon to play at the villagers' Christmas concert, the Rio Trio offer a lift to a girl, unaware she is a single mum taking her baby home to see her parents for the holiday. However, the first the band know about the infant is when the girl disappears, leaving them to look after it. Meanwhile, as the concert gets into full swing, local barmaid Gina takes centre stage, hoping to impress all and sundry with her rendition of Winter Wonderland. Tricia Penrose, Derek Fowlds, Geoffrey Hughes, William Simons and Mark Jordon star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Steve spends another night with Kim. Kelly starts a fight with Lyn Hutchinson. Zoe phones the police when Sophie realises her overnight bag has been stolen

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff embarks on a fitness regime. Zak makes Butch swear that he was not at Zoe's the previous night. Billy turns up unannounced at the Sugdens' home to see Andy

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Becky stands up for Jamie when Lauren teases him about his dad being in prison. Ken tells Deirdre he fears Sue is attracted to him. Tricia is annoyed that she cannot divorce Carl

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Raquel tells Curly if he wants children he needs to show commitment. Ken tries to get out of going to the conference with Sue. Roy catches Jamie skiving off school

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Community spirit in the village of Morton Fendle is dealt a blow by the collapse of a craft centre in which many locals had invested - and Alan Hollingsworth, the businessman behind the project, has become a public hate figure. Barnaby investigates when Hollingsworth's wife disappears, and seems to have been kidnapped. However, when Hollingsworth is subsequently murdered and his wife reappears safe and sound, he suspects an elaborate scheme. Roger Allam guest stars

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack tackles what promises to be the most sensitive and unenviable investigation of his entire career - deciding whether a man with Down's syndrome is capable of committing murder. David Jason stars as the caustic copper, with Bruce Alexander and Matthew Marsh

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth falls asleep during a flight from Paris to London, while a female passenger is murdered by a poisoned dart, leaving few clues to the killer's identity. Poirot soon discovers she was a money-lender, which may provide a motive for the killing. The investigation is turned on its head, however, when the victim's illegitimate daughter comes forward to claim the inheritance. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with David Firth, Jenny Downham and John Bleasdale

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. A professional hitman bursts into the luxurious family home of accountant Keith Rothwell and shoots him in cold blood. Banks is instantly struck by the strange reactions of the victim's family and evidence comes to light that Rothwell has been living a secret double life. Now investigating the murder of a victim with two identities, Banks and Morton discover a possible link to a major money-laundering operation and are plunged into a high-profile fraud investigation with political ramifications

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates the murder of her old friend Father Gorman, using a list of names sent by the priest moments before his death to bring the guilty party to justice. Following a trail of clues to London, Marple is drawn to the Pale Horse Inn - where she discovers a sinister world of occult practices. Drama, starring Julia McKenzie in the title role, with Lynda Baron, Pauline Collins, Nicholas Parsons and Neil Pearson

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack tackles what promises to be the most sensitive and unenviable investigation of his entire career - deciding whether a man with Down's syndrome is capable of committing murder. David Jason stars as the caustic copper, with Bruce Alexander and Matthew Marsh

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is in for a shock when he finds out who Jean has enlisted to decorate Mike's bedroom
